title: Choose a side to simplify
text: It is often easier to start with the more complex side of the identity and simplify it to match the simpler side.

title: Identify the more complex side
text: Which side of the identity, $$csc**{2}\theta-2$$ or $$\frac{\cos(2\theta)}{sin**{2}\theta}$$ appears more complex and might be easier to simplify?

title: Apply a double-angle identity
text: Recall the double-angle identities for cosine. Which one involves sine squared?

title: Simplify the expression
text: Split the fraction and use a reciprocal identity to simplify the expression to match the other side of the identity.

title: Final simplification
text: Simplify $$\frac{1-2\sin**{2}\theta}{sin**{2}\theta}$$ by splitting the fraction and applying the reciprocal identity for $$1/sin**{2}\theta$$.
